excellent: Amazing focus during all of our lessons. They never miss a thing when instructions are given. This great listening helps them learn very quickly.
Always pays close attention to the teacher and friends. They are a great listener every single day. This skill makes them a wonderful student.
Shows great focus whenever we are learning. They follow all instructions perfectly on the first try. Their listening skills are truly excellent.
Listens beautifully and respects whoever is speaking. They learn new information very quickly. It is wonderful to teach such a focused learner.
Pays full attention during all parts of the lesson. They understand instructions quickly and follow them well. This excellent listening skill helps them perform very well.
Always focuses on the speaker and listens carefully. They rarely need instructions to be repeated. This is a very important skill and they do it perfectly.
Listens with great care during all classroom activities. They remember information well because they pay close attention. This strong skill supports all areas of their learning.
A truly focused student who gives full attention to every lesson. They follow directions the first time they hear them. Their listening is one of their best skills.
Always quiet and focused when someone is speaking. They take in everything and remember it very well. This strong focus helps them do excellent work in class.
Their listening skills are some of the best in the class. They understand new ideas quickly because they pay such good attention. I am very proud of their focus.

Working hard to become a better listener in class. They are slowly learning to follow directions more closely. Keep practicing this very important skill.

Starting to look at the speaker more often during class time. They are trying to follow instructions without needing help. These are wonderful steps forward.

Improving listening skills will help them in all areas of learning. I encourage them to look at the speaker when listening. Small changes in focus will make a big difference.

I know they can become a better listener with a little more effort. Trying to look at the teacher when they speak is a great start. I believe in their ability to improve.

Always willing to give something a try, even if it is hard. They believe in their own ability to succeed. This self-confidence is a very important quality.

Makes very few errors in both speaking and writing. They check their own work and fix problems quickly. This careful approach helps them produce excellent English.

good: Showing good control of English grammar and spelling. They usually fix their own little mistakes. This is a great sign of learning on their own.
Makes very few mistakes during our daily activities. They are doing a good job using the rules. Keep up this careful and correct work.
Generally very correct when using new English words. They understand the basics and use them correctly. This correctness helps them communicate well.
Produces good work with only minor, expected errors. They are learning to spot their mistakes easily. I am happy with their overall correctness.
Shows good correctness when using English in class. They make only minor mistakes and are improving. This good control of the language supports their overall progress.
Generally uses correct grammar and vocabulary in their work. They are good at noticing and fixing small errors. I am happy with the correctness they are showing.
Produces work with a good level of correctness overall. They are building a stronger understanding of grammar rules. Keep up this careful and correct approach.
Uses English words and grammar correctly most of the time. They are getting better at checking their own work. I am happy with the way they use the language.
Makes only small mistakes that are normal for this level. They are learning to notice and fix their own errors. This is a very positive sign of progress.
Their English work shows good understanding of the rules we learn. They are careful with their spelling and grammar. Keep up this great effort in being correct.
developing: Learning so well from every little mistake made. They are starting to understand the grammar rules better. Keep up the good work and patience.
Slowly reducing their common mistakes over time. They are trying harder to remember the right words. These improvements in correctness are great to see.
Beginning to catch their own errors during practice. They are taking good steps toward speaking correctly. Let's keep practicing those hard rules.
Making steady progress in using correct English. Mistakes are getting fewer as they learn more. Keep focusing and practicing carefully.
Correctness is improving as they learn more grammar rules. They are starting to catch and fix their own mistakes. With more practice, their care will grow.
Making progress in reducing errors in their English. They are working hard to understand and use the rules correctly. These improvements in correctness are good.
Slowly becoming more aware of common errors they make. They are putting in good effort to use correct forms. I am confident their correctness will continue to improve.
Getting better at using the right grammar and words each week. They are learning from their mistakes and trying again. This is exactly how learners improve.
Starting to understand which mistakes they make most often. They are working to fix these common errors one by one. I am proud of the effort they are putting in.
Their work is becoming more correct as they practice more. They are slowly learning which rules to follow in English. With more practice, their mistakes will become fewer.
needs_work: Every mistake is just a new chance to learn and grow. We will keep practicing the basic grammar rules together. Don't worry, they will get it soon!
Let's work on slowing down to fix our little errors. Making mistakes is a normal part of learning. We will practice until they get it right.
I encourage them to focus a bit more on correctness. Reviewing the rules will help stop these common errors. I am here to help them practice well.
They will improve their correctness with more practice. Mistakes just show us what we need to learn next. Let's keep trying our very best.
Reviewing main grammar rules regularly will help reduce errors. I encourage them to check their work carefully before finishing. With regular practice, their correctness will improve.
Making mistakes is a normal and important part of learning. The important thing is to keep practicing and trying. Their correctness will grow stronger with every lesson.
They will benefit from focusing on one grammar rule at a time. Practicing these rules in sentences will make a big difference. I am here to support their progress in correctness.
Mistakes are a normal part of learning a new language. I encourage them to look at their errors as a chance to learn something new. With practice, they will make fewer mistakes.
Let's focus on the most common mistakes and practice fixing them together. Learning one rule at a time is the best way to improve. I know they can do it with a little effort.
Every learner makes mistakes and that is perfectly okay. I encourage them to take their time and check their work carefully. With patience and practice, their English will become more correct.
